name: mighty-max
description: MiniMax M-series language models for VS Code Chat (BYOK)

mightymax-head.description: Mighty Max aviator glyph (used as the status-bar item icon).

apiKey.description: MiniMax API key. Stored in SecretStorage by the `Mighty Max: Manage MiniMax` command. Required for the model picker to surface this vendor.

mightyMax.baseUrl.description: MiniMax API base URL. The API key is never stored here — it lives in SecretStorage.

mightyMax.firstByteTimeoutMs.description: Watchdog on time-to-first-response, in milliseconds. If MiniMax accepts the connection but sends no response headers within this budget, the attempt is aborted and retried with backoff; exhausted retries surface a 'stall' error instead of hanging until you cancel. Healthy requests return headers in single-digit seconds even on large cached prompts. Changes apply to the next request — no reload needed.

mightyMax.idleTimeoutMs.description: Watchdog on mid-stream silence, in milliseconds. If an open response stream delivers no bytes for this long, it is cut with a 'stall' error (and retried automatically when nothing had streamed yet). This measures gaps between bytes, not total request time — long responses that keep streaming are never cut. Changes apply to the next request — no reload needed.

mightyMax.logLevel.description: Log level for Mighty Max. Logs never contain the API key, Authorization header, or request/response bodies.

mightyMax.enableSmartToolFiltering.description: Smart tool filtering. When enabled, the agent's tool set is capped at `mightyMax.maxTools` (default 64); history-referenced tools and a pin list of always-on built-ins (`copilot_*`, `run_in_terminal`, `apply_patch`, `grep_search`, `file_search`, `semantic_search`) always survive the cap. The full VS Code tool set is ~83 entries on a typical install — keeping the wire payload small directly reduces first-turn latency on MiniMax M3 (Anthropic endpoint) where tool-schema size dominates the cold-cache build. Set to `false` to forward every tool verbatim; the pin list and history-referenced set are still honored.

mightyMax.maxTools.description: Maximum number of tools to send to the model when smart filtering is enabled. M3 handles 64 cleanly; Copilot Chat's virtual-tool grouper handles larger sets at the chat-UI level, not here. Lower this if your provider enforces a smaller cap.

mightyMax.alwaysIncludeTools.description: Tools to keep regardless of relevance scoring when filtering is enabled. Supports exact names (run_in_terminal), prefix pins (copilot_ matches every copilot_* built-in), and substring matches (grep matches grep_search, grep_file_contents). Tools referenced by the current request's tool_use/tool_result history are also pinned automatically.

enumDescriptions: Filter based on semantic relevance to the current prompt (keyword matching)
Filter based on historical usage patterns (frequently-used tools prioritized)
Combine relevance and usage scoring for balanced filtering
mightyMax.toolFilterStrategy.description: Strategy for selecting which tools to include when filtering is enabled.

mightyMax.systemPrompt.description: Optional system prompt sent on every M3 request. When empty, Mighty Max uses a short preamble that nudges M3 to plan before the first tool call. The prompt is sanitized for UTF-16 surrogate code points and sent with `cache_control: ephemeral` so it is reused across requests.

scripts.test:unit: npm run test:unit:vscode-stub && node node_modules/@vscode/test-cli/out/bin.mjs --label unit
